questions.question: What is the 'Trust Pincer' strategy in the context of LinkedIn lead generation?

options.text: Using automated tools to send 500 messages a day.
Combining direct outreach/connection with public authority building through content.
Hiding your profile so only customers can see it.
Asking friends and family to like your posts.

questions.explanation: The Trust Pincer uses two 'arms': one that reaches out directly to prospective customers and another that builds public trust so they know who you are when you reach out.

questions.question: Why is LinkedIn considered a 'Bridge' channel compared to cold email and SEO?

options.text: Because it's cheaper than paying for a website.
Because it allows you to scale your personality like social media while being as targeted as cold email.
Because it was built by a bridge engineer.
Because it doesn't require any effort from the founder.

questions.explanation: LinkedIn offers the best of both worlds: high intentionality (like cold email) and high trust signals/authority (like SEO/Social).

questions.question: What is the most important metric for a solo founder's success on LinkedIn?

options.text: The number of followers they have.
The number of likes on their most viral post.
The quality of the pipeline and relationships with their Ideal Customer Profile (ICP).
The color of their profile picture.

questions.explanation: Virality is often a vanity metric. For founders, LinkedIn is an acquisition engine, which means success is measured by the business results it generates.

questions.question: Why is being a 'Solo Practitioner' an advantage on LinkedIn?

options.text: Because you don't have to pay for employees.
Because people prefer to buy from authentic people who understand their struggle rather than faceless corporations.
Because solo founders can post more often than companies.
Because there is no competition for solo founders on LinkedIn.

questions.explanation: Trust is easier to build with a person than a brand. Your practitioner voice and unique 'Build in Public' journey are powerful signals that cut through noise.

questions.question: If your last 50 connections are not your Ideal Customer Profile (ICP), what does this indicate about your engine?

options.text: You are winning at networking.
Your engine is 'Imprecise' and your reach is being wasted on the wrong audience.
You need to buy LinkedIn Premium.
LinkedIn is the wrong platform for your business.

questions.explanation: Precise targeting is the foundation of a lead generation engine. If you are connecting with the wrong people, your content will never reach the right customers.

questions.question: Look at your own LinkedIn history. Are you currently using it as a 'Resume Graveyard' or a 'Lead Generation Engine'? If you wanted to move toward the 'Trust Pincer' strategy today, what is the #1 change you'd make to your profile or posting habit?
promptHints: Think about your headline and who it targets.
Consider if you're sharing 'Practitioner' insights or generic tips.
Evaluate the quality of your most recent connection requests.

title: LinkedIn as Lead Generation Channel - Quiz
